title: BOSS BATTLE — The Structure Awakens
narrativeIntro:
The largest structure in the third region — a complex of interconnected chambers, each dependent on the others — has been dark since you arrived. It's an application. A real one, with routes, components, services, and state management. The builders left it broken.

Three components. One service. Three bugs. 60 minutes to bring the whole system online.
narrativeOutro:
The structure awakens. Not suddenly — in stages. First the routing map illuminates, then the services pulse, then the components render one by one until the entire complex is alive, glowing with the warm gold of something complete and functioning.

Three clusters active. One remains. The final region is where mastery lives.
phmConnectionNote: A pivotal moment where everything Ryland built is tested. Your Angular knowledge faces its test.
conceptsPreview: Boss battle — debug a broken Angular application across service, component, and routing layers

challenges.title: The Structure Awakens
challenges.narrativeHook: Service is broken. Component reads the wrong property. Navigation points nowhere. Fix all three.
challenges.instructions:
This application has three bugs across three files.

**Bug 1 — `catalog.component.ts`:** `filteredStations` filters on the wrong property. It should filter on `name`, not `id`.

**Bug 2 — `detail.component.ts`:** `displayTitle` shows the wrong field. It should show `station.name`, not `station.id`.

**Bug 3 — `app.service.ts`:** The `getById()` method compares with `===` but `id` from the route is a string. Coerce it to a number.

All three tests must pass.
challenges.starterCode: // Open the tabs above to see the three files with bugs.

files.name: catalog.component.ts
detail.component.ts
app.service.ts

tests.description: CatalogComponent: filteredStations with 'alpha' should match by name
tests.testCode: const c = new CatalogComponent(); c.searchTerm = 'alpha'; if (c.filteredStations.length !== 1 || c.filteredStations[0].name !== 'Relay Alpha') throw new Error(`filteredStations('alpha') should return Relay Alpha, got ${JSON.stringify(c.filteredStations)}`);

const d = new DetailComponent(); d.station = { id: 2, name: 'Probe Beta' }; if (d.displayTitle !== 'Station: Probe Beta') throw new Error(`displayTitle should be 'Station: Probe Beta', got '${d.displayTitle}'`);

tests.description: AppService: getById should work with string id from route params
tests.testCode: const s = new AppService(); const result = s.getById('2'); if (!result || result.name !== 'Probe Beta') throw new Error(`getById('2') should return Probe Beta, got ${JSON.stringify(result)}`);

explainGate.prompt: Describe each of the three bugs you fixed. For bug 3, explain why '2' === 2 is false in JavaScript and how Number() solves it.

hints: Bug 2: Change this.station.id to this.station.name in displayTitle
Bug 3: Route params are always strings. '2' === 2 is false (strict equality, different types). Use Number(id) to coerce: s.id === Number(id)
